# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert

Prevent command injection.
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def create
    @image = Image.new(params[:file]) # Assuming you have a model named 'Image' with attribute :uploaded_file, type is :string (or whatever your file uploading method uses) and accepts the uploaded image as an argument in initialize function or similar way to handle it properly for Rails
    @image.save! if params[:commit] == "Upload" # Save changes before processing because we want a real-time preview of what's going on, not just saving without validation/processing (which would be pointless) 
    `convert -quality $QUALITY_LEVEL %IMAGE% --format '%FORMAT%%[orientation]' resized:%FINAL_FILENAME%.jpg #{Rails.root}/app/assets/images/logo-white@2x~retina.png" system(command)`
    redirect_to @image, notice: 'Image was successfully created.'
